title: Daybreak: Tools for securing every organization in the world
description: OpenAI launched Daybreak on June 22, a cybersecurity program built around GPT-5.5-Cyber, a new specialized model optimized for discovering and patching software vulnerabilities at machine speed. The model scores 85.6% on CyberGym and 39.5% on ExploitGym, outperforming the general-purpose GPT-5.5, and has already been applied to vulnerabilities in major browsers, operating systems, and network infrastructure. The initiative also includes an update to the Codex Security plugin to prevent new vulnerabilities from reaching production.

title: SpaceX signs computing power deal with open-source AI startup Reflection worth up to $6.3 billion
description: SpaceX signed a compute agreement worth up to $6.3 billion with Reflection, an open-source AI startup, granting access to capacity at its Colossus 1 data center in Memphis, Tennessee. The deal follows SpaceX's existing $1.25 billion-per-month arrangement with Anthropic and signals that Colossus is emerging as a major third-party AI compute provider. Reflection focuses on developing open-weight frontier models accessible to the broader research community.

title: Anthropic releases Mythos-like AI model to the public, Claude Fable 5
description: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, a frontier model with capabilities approaching its restricted Mythos Preview, to enterprise customers and paid subscribers on June 9. The model includes safety guardrails that block outputs in high-risk domains such as advanced cybersecurity and biology, enabling broader public access than Mythos. Starting June 23, Fable 5 requires usage credits at $10 per million input tokens and $50 per million output tokens for all Claude subscribers.

title: SpaceX Leases Colossus 1 Data Center to Anthropic After Internal AI Hurdles
description: Bloomberg reported that SpaceX began leasing Colossus 1 to Anthropic after internal SpaceX AI teams struggled to fully utilize the facility's more than 220,000 Nvidia GPUs. Under the deal, Anthropic pays $1.25 billion per month through May 2029 and also expressed interest in jointly developing gigawatts of space-based compute with SpaceX. The reporting reveals that commercial demand, rather than SpaceX's own AI ambitions, is now the primary driver of Colossus capacity.

title: Microsoft unveils new AI models to lessen reliance on OpenAI and lower costs for developers
description: At Microsoft Build 2026, Microsoft announced its first proprietary AI models: MAI-Code-1-Flash, which converts natural-language descriptions into application source code and is rolling out across GitHub Copilot and Visual Studio Code, and MAI Transcribe 1.5, which supports 43 languages with near-state-of-the-art accuracy. The launches mark Microsoft's push to compete at the model layer and reduce its dependency on OpenAI for core developer products. Both models are positioned as cost-efficient alternatives for enterprise developers.

title: Anthropic confidentially submits draft S-1 to the SEC
description: Anthropic confidentially submitted a draft Form S-1 registration statement to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on June 1, beginning the formal IPO process at a valuation near $965 billion following a $65 billion Series H round. The filing puts Anthropic ahead of OpenAI, which was also preparing its own confidential IPO submission, in what analysts are calling the most significant AI capital market event since the dot-com era. Anthropic reported a revenue run rate of $47 billion, up from $10 billion the prior year, driven by surging enterprise demand for Claude.
